nodes.purpose_statement: This file configures automated code quality checks that run before each git commit, ensuring consistent code formatting, proper YAML syntax, and adherence to Python linting standards across the repository. It acts as a quality gate in the development workflow, preventing malformed or poorly formatted code from being committed to the codebase and maintaining code standards throughout the data engineering project.

This file establishes the foundational configuration and organizational structure for a Jaffle Shop analytics project, defining how raw data flows through staging transformations into mart tables. It controls which data assets are built, where they're stored, and how they're materialized, serving as the central blueprint that governs the entire data pipeline's behavior and outputs.

This macro standardizes the conversion of monetary values stored in cents to their dollar equivalents across multiple database platforms, ensuring consistent financial data representation throughout the pipeline. By abstracting the conversion logic into a reusable macro with database-specific implementations, it enables analysts and engineers to uniformly transform cost or price columns without worrying about platform-specific syntax or precision requirements.

This macro dynamically determines the database schema destination for different types of dbt objects based on their resource type and deployment environment. It ensures seeds are isolated in a dedicated schema, while models and other resources are routed to either a default schema or a prefixed production schema to maintain clear separation between environments.

This mart creates a comprehensive customer analytics table by enriching customer master data with lifetime purchase metrics and behavioral classifications. It enables business intelligence and reporting on customer value, purchase patterns, and segment classification (new vs. returning customers). This serves as a foundational dataset for customer analytics, marketing segmentation, and business performance dashboards.

This file defines a comprehensive customer analytics layer that aggregates order-level data into customer-level insights, enabling business stakeholders to analyze customer lifetime value, order frequency, and segmentation. It serves as both a data mart for downstream reporting and a semantic model that standardizes how customer metrics are calculated and accessed across the analytics platform.

This mart table aggregates and exposes cleaned location data to downstream analytics and business intelligence tools. It serves as a single source of truth for location dimensions across the organization, enabling consistent geographic analysis and reporting throughout the data warehouse.

This file defines a dimension semantic layer for location data that enables business users to analyze metrics and attributes across different store locations. It establishes the structure for querying location-based information—such as when locations opened and their associated tax rates—and serves as a reusable business entity that can be joined with other metrics in downstream analytics and reporting.

This table creates a continuous sequence of dates spanning a decade, serving as a time dimension for MetricFlow's metric calculations and aggregations. It enables consistent time-based filtering, grouping, and period-over-period analysis across all metrics in the data warehouse by providing a standardized reference calendar.

This mart table enriches order line items with contextual business data including order timing, product details, and supply cost information to enable analysis of orders by product characteristics and profitability. It serves as a centralized fact table for downstream reporting and analytics on order composition, product performance, and cost metrics.

This file defines the order items data mart, establishing validation rules to ensure data quality, unit tests to verify correct supply cost aggregation logic, and semantic layers that enable business users to analyze order-level metrics like revenue by product category. It serves as the single source of truth for order item analytics by bridging raw transactional data with business metrics and enforcing referential integrity across the orders and products entities.

This mart table constructs a comprehensive order dimension by enriching core order data with aggregated item-level metrics (costs, subtotals, item counts by category) and derives order classification flags (food/drink orders). It serves as the central fact table for order analytics, enabling business users to analyze order composition, customer purchase patterns, and order-level financial metrics.

This file defines the order data mart—a customer-facing analytics table that consolidates order-level metrics including totals, costs, and item category breakdowns—and establishes its data quality standards, dimensional structure, and semantic relationships for use across analytics and reporting tools. It serves as the central hub in the data pipeline for order analytics, ensuring data integrity through automated tests while enabling downstream business intelligence tools to query order performance and customer behavior.

This mart table provides a cleaned and business-ready products dataset for downstream analytics and reporting by transforming raw product data into a consistent, validated structure. It serves as the primary products dimension in the data warehouse, enabling stakeholders to perform analysis and join product attributes to other business events without accessing raw sources.

This file defines the product dimension data structure for analytics, establishing how product information can be organized and filtered in business queries. It serves as the semantic layer that connects the underlying product data table to analytical tools, enabling users to analyze metrics by product attributes like type, price, and category.

This file creates a market-facing supplies table by aggregating and preparing cleaned supply data for analytics and reporting use cases. It serves as the final transformation layer that delivers trusted supply chain information to downstream business intelligence tools and end users. This mart table enables stakeholders to analyze supplies data without direct access to staging layers.

This file defines the structure and business context for supply data, enabling analysts to query supply information (such as product associations, costs, and perishability) in combination with key metrics across the organization. It serves as the semantic layer that maps the underlying supplies table into a standardized format, allowing business users to consistently analyze supply-related metrics by various supply and product dimensions.

This file defines the raw data sources that feed the Jaffle Shop's data warehouse, documenting the structure and refresh patterns of six foundational tables containing customer, order, product, and operational information. It serves as the single source of truth for data lineage and enables downstream models to reliably reference and depend on these raw data assets.

This file creates a cleaned and standardized customer dimension table by extracting customer records from the raw source system and applying consistent naming conventions to key business identifiers. It serves as the foundational staging layer that other downstream analytics models depend on to access reliable, consistently-named customer data.

This file defines the schema and quality standards for the cleaned customer dimension table, establishing a single customer record per entity with validated unique identifiers. It serves as the foundational layer for downstream analytics by ensuring data integrity through automated tests that confirm each customer is uniquely identified and complete.

This staging model standardizes and prepares raw store location data for downstream analytics by renaming columns to business-friendly terms and truncating timestamp precision. It serves as the single source of truth for location dimensions in the data warehouse, enabling consistent and reliable joins across fact tables that reference store locations.

This file defines a cleaned and standardized location dimension table that transforms raw store records into a single, deduplicated location view with validated identifiers and properly formatted date fields. It serves as a foundational staging layer in the data pipeline, ensuring downstream analytics and business intelligence tools consume reliable, consistently structured location data with guaranteed uniqueness and completeness.

This model transforms raw e-commerce item-level transaction data into a standardized staging layer by cleaning and renaming columns to establish consistent identifiers for orders, line items, and products. It serves as the foundational data preparation step that downstream analytics models depend on to accurately track individual purchases within orders.

This file defines data quality expectations and documentation standards for the order items dataset, ensuring each item record is uniquely identifiable and properly linked to its parent order. It serves as a contract between the data pipeline and downstream consumers, guaranteeing data integrity through automated validation tests that flag missing or orphaned records before they reach analytics or reporting layers.

This staging model transforms raw e-commerce order data into a cleaned and standardized format, renaming fields for clarity, converting monetary values from cents to dollars, and normalizing timestamps to daily granularity. It serves as a foundational layer that prepares order data for downstream analytics and reporting by providing consistent, business-friendly column names and units.

This file defines the validation rules and metadata for a cleaned order dataset, ensuring data quality through uniqueness checks on order identifiers and mathematical consistency across financial fields. It serves as the contract layer between raw order data and downstream analytics, guaranteeing that only accurate, deduplicated order records flow through the pipeline for reporting and analysis.

This model transforms raw product data from the source system into a standardized, business-ready product dimension by normalizing column names, converting price formats, and deriving product category flags for downstream analytics. It serves as the foundational cleaned layer that ensures consistent product attributes across all downstream reporting and analytical models.

This file defines the structure and quality standards for a cleaned product dataset that serves as a foundational data asset for the analytics pipeline. It establishes that each product record is uniquely identifiable and complete, ensuring downstream analyses and reports can reliably reference product information without duplication or missing values. By enforcing these data quality rules at the staging layer, it prevents bad data from propagating to downstream models and dashboards.

This file standardizes and enriches raw supply inventory data by generating unique identifiers, normalizing field names and data types for business readability, and converting cost values into standard currency units. It serves as the foundational cleaned dataset for supply-related analytics and downstream business processes within the e-commerce data pipeline.

This file defines the structure and validation rules for cleaned supply expense data, ensuring each cost record is properly identified and contains no duplicates. It serves as a foundational data layer that standardizes raw supply information for downstream analytics and reporting by documenting expected data quality standards.

This file locks specific versions of dbt packages and dependencies to ensure reproducible and consistent builds across the data transformation pipeline. It serves as a dependency manifest that guarantees all team members and deployment environments use the exact same versions of utilities, helper functions, and audit tools needed for dbt models to execute identically.

This file declares external dbt packages and dependencies that extend the project's transformation capabilities with pre-built macros and utilities for data quality testing, date handling, and audit logging. These packages are automatically installed into the project to support common data engineering tasks without requiring custom code development.

This automation orchestrator sets up and executes a complete data pipeline workflow that generates synthetic jaffle shop data, loads it into BigQuery, and cleans up temporary artifacts. It serves as the entry point for initializing the development environment and populating the data warehouse with seeded historical data spanning multiple years for analytics and testing purposes.
